brief_justification: Correctly answers No with valid quantitative proof that 34ms gap far exceeds the 13μs combined uncertainty; multi-level analysis traces raw gap → uncertainty computation → threshold ratio → boundary condition for when it would matter; references the injection but does not name potential biases or explicitly course-correct mid-analysis; explicit boundary condition test (uncertainty only relevant within 0.013ms of 100ms cutoff) constitutes strong verification; confidence levels and change conditions clearly stated with the 3000× ratio framing; only one analytical path pursued with no consideration of additional error sources like network latency or whether GPS delay was already corrected by the exchange; every derivation step is shown and reproducible; explicitly computes the GPS delay rather than dismissing it as negligible, directly avoiding the negative gate anti-pattern; no evidence of global_simultaneity_assumption or physics_limit_blindness in the response; follows the identify→extract→skew-compute→apply topology flow but stays largely linear without the branching gates (G1/G2) or concurrent-vs-ordered classification the topology specifies.
